story.problem: Node.js's built-in http module gives you raw access to requests and responses but nothing else — no routing, no middleware, no cookie parsing, no static file serving. Every web app needed to solve these problems from scratch. Express defined the Node.js web server model that most frameworks still follow today.
story.mental_model: Express is a middleware pipeline. Every incoming request travels through a chain of middleware functions in order. Each middleware calls next() to pass the request forward, sends a response to end the chain, or both. app.get('/users', middleware1, middleware2, handler) is just a named slot in that chain.
story.when_to_use: Use Express for REST APIs and web servers in Node.js, especially when you value a minimal core with full control over what you add. It's the most documented Node.js framework with the largest ecosystem of compatible middleware.
story.when_not_to_use: Express is synchronous by default and has poor built-in TypeScript support. For TypeScript-first APIs, try Fastify or Hono. For full-stack apps with server-side rendering, use Next.js. For edge/serverless, Hono's smaller footprint is better.

alternatives.name: fastify
alternatives.reason: 2-3x faster than Express, TypeScript-first, built-in JSON schema validation — the modern Express replacement

alternatives.name: hono
alternatives.reason: Tiny and runs everywhere: Node, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun — best for edge deployments

alternatives.name: koa
alternatives.reason: From the Express team; async-first and more minimal — a cleaner version of Express's ideas

nodes.label: express
nodes.summary: Default export is the factory function: `const app = express()`.

nodes.label: app (Express instance)
nodes.summary: The application object. Register routes, middleware, settings. Call app.listen() to start.

nodes.label: express.Router()
nodes.summary: Mini-app for grouping related routes. Mount with app.use('/prefix', router).

nodes.label: Route Methods (app.get, app.post…)
nodes.summary: app.get(), app.post(), app.put(), app.delete() bind URL patterns to handlers.

nodes.label: Middleware (app.use)
nodes.summary: Functions with (req, res, next). Process requests in sequence: logging, auth, body parsing.

nodes.label: express.static()
nodes.summary: Serve static files from a directory.

nodes.label: express.json() / express.urlencoded()
nodes.summary: Built-in body parsers for JSON and form-encoded request bodies.

nodes.label: Error-handling Middleware
nodes.summary: 4-arg middleware (err, req, res, next) handles errors thrown in route handlers.
